summary: The development marks a turnaround for SpiceJet in the legal battle after the airline had earlier argued that it lacked the liquidity to make an immediate cash deposit and had sought permission to furnish a commercial property in Gurugram as security

title: Tata Capital enters gold loans with controlling stake in Kerala-based Yogakshemam Loans
summary: Tata Group's financial services arm will acquire an 88.6% stake in Yogakshemam Loans, marking its entry into the secured gold loan business as it expands its retail lending franchise.

title: SC upholds Centre's mining royalty formula, rejects Kirloskar's challenge
summary: The Supreme Court's ruling is expected to provide certainty to the mining sector by preserving the existing royalty framework, and also comes as a major relief to the Centre and mineral-rich states

title: Have India’s top hotel rates peaked? Operators say there’s still room, but not everyone agrees
summary: Five-star and luxury hotels in India continue to find room for rate increases compared with global prices. While some believe city hotel rates may be nearing a ceiling, resort pricing holds more potential for upward movement.

title: HDFC Bank trims workforce by over 3,300 in FY26, says tech will elevate, not replace people
summary: India's largest private sector lender, HDFC Bank, reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees during FY26. Managing Director and CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan said in the annual report that employees must adapt as the organisation evolves.

title: BNPL impact on credit score: 5 key facts every borrower should know before using buy now, pay later
summary: A buy now, pay later facility can help build a strong credit history when used responsibly. Timely repayments, controlled borrowing, and avoiding defaults are crucial. Learn how BNPL services affect credit scores, borrowing ability, and financial health with key tips for users.

title: Presumptive taxation: Who should opt for it & who should avoid it? Experts explain
summary: The presumptive taxation scheme offers a simpler way to compute taxable income, but it may not suit every taxpayer. Here's what businesses and professionals should consider before choosing it.

title: How much EPF can you withdraw? Check it online with these simple steps.
summary: The new rules compress a dozen complicated advance clauses into three broad categories while requiring a mandatory 25% retirement reserve.

title: Bought a car worth over  ₹10 lakh? Here's how to claim your 1% TCS refund
summary: If you have bought a car worth more than  ₹10 lakh, you would have paid a TCS of 1% to the dealer. This is not an additional tax but a tax credit that can be adjusted against your total income tax liability or claimed as a refund while filing the ITR.&nbsp;

title: Filing ITR-1 this year? These new disclosure requirements could affect your return
summary: From reporting income from two house properties to furnishing additional details for donations and rental income, the latest ITR-1 utility brings several changes. Here's what taxpayers need to know before filing.

title: Retirement, child education or wealth creation: Does every SIP in your portfolio have a purpose?
summary: Every SIP should be linked to a specific financial goal, such as retirement, a child's education, buying a house, or wealth creation. The expert suggested that the number of funds you own or their recent returns matter less than whether each SIP is aligned with a clear financial objective.

title: Income Tax Act 2025 simplifies presumptive tax—but one change could hurt taxpayers
summary: The new Income Tax Act largely simplifies presumptive taxation rules, but one change could restrict taxpayers from setting off certain losses against presumptive income.

title: RBI new ombudsman rules: Here's why you should act quickly
summary: The new framework empowers the ombudsman to issue interim, non-binding advisories to the regulated entity, requiring it to take action for full or partial resolution to enable quicker settlement.

title: ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund portfolio: 3 new stocks added, GAIL among 4 exits in June
summary: India's largest large cap mutual fund shuffled its portfolio in June by adding three stocks and exiting four. Tata Steel saw the biggest increase in holdings, while GAIL featured among the complete exits.
